steps.title: Factor the denominator and multiply by the common denominator
steps.content:
Notice that $x^2 - 9 = (x+3)(x-3)$. Rewriting the equation:
$$\frac{4x^2}{(x+3)(x-3)} - \frac{2x}{x + 3} = \frac{1}{x -3}$$
The LCD is $(x+3)(x-3)$. Multiply both sides by this to eliminate denominators:
$$4x^2 - 2x(x-3) = x+3$$

steps.title: Factor the quadratic
steps.content:
We need two numbers that multiply to $(2)(-3) = -6$ and add to $5$. Those are $6$ and $-1$.
$$2x^2 + 6x - x - 3 = 0$$
$$2x(x+3) - 1(x+3) = 0$$
$$(2x-1)(x+3) = 0$$
So $x = \frac{1}{2}$ or $x = -3$.

steps.title: Check domain restrictions
steps.content: The denominators give restrictions: $x \neq -3$ and $x \neq 3$. Since $x = -3$ makes the original equation undefined, it is extraneous. The valid solution is $x = \frac{1}{2}$. That matches choice C — C is correct.
